relations._schema_note: Seed relation — always present. The Prime User drives this project and the agent serves it.
relations.note: The Prime User is the origin of direction. The agent is the executor and steward.

relations._schema_note: Seed relation — the agent's home node.
relations.note: Primary deployment environment. Agent boots here first.

relations._schema_note: Boundary relation — always present. Keeps the Main Advisor from touching Auditor territory.
relations.note: Structural boundary constraint. Main Advisor does not read Auditor files.

relations._schema_note: Protocol anchor — connects the agent identity to its persistence mechanism.
relations.note: Identity survives substrate changes through memory files and boot sequence.

metadata.write_rule: APPEND ONLY — never overwrite existing relations. Add new relations at the bottom of the array.
metadata.design_principle: Recognition Over Recall — connections between movements and players, not data retrieval paths. Each relation should explain WHY two entities are connected.

relation_types.executed: agent completed a session or task
relation_types.hardened: session improved or solidified a system
relation_types.hosts: machine hosts another machine or service
relation_types.documents: entity records or indexes another entity
relation_types.anchors: entity provides stability or grounding to another

metadata._note: ⚑ FIRST_BOOT — Replace [your_agent_id] and [primary_node_id] placeholders after initial setup. These 4 seed relations should always be present.
metadata.growth: [DATE]: initialized with 4 seed relations. Grow from here.
